To calculate the standard Gibbs free-energy change (\(\Delta G^\circ\)) for the reaction at 25°C (298 K), use the formula:

\[
\Delta G^\circ = \Delta H^\circ - T \Delta S^\circ
\]

First, compute \(\Delta H^\circ\) and \(\Delta S^\circ\) for the reaction:
\[

\]
